untranslatable_reason: Signal requires measurement of trader's competitive advantage dimensions (product knowledge, speed, structural positioning) relative to market participants. These are qualitative, behavioral factors that cannot be expressed using only price/volume technical indicators. The rule requires: (1) detection of trader's edge type and magnitude, (2) competitive parity assessment vs 'the floor/the trade', (3) dynamic position sizing based on advantage score. None of these can be quantified from the allowed indicator set without explicit external data feeds on market microstructure, participant activity, or trader capability metrics.
translation_notes: This is a meta-rule about WHEN NOT TO TRADE rather than a directional trading signal. It defines position eligibility criteria based on comparative advantage and market structure dominance. The parameters suggest a scoring system (edge_type_weighting, positional_advantage_score) that requires external inputs beyond technical analysis. Recommend: (1) implement as a pre-trade filter/gate rather than a backtest rule, (2) require explicit advantage assessment inputs, or (3) proxy with volatility regime and market participation metrics if available.
